input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:

Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-03T00:53:38.920Z</current_datetime>

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference — Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns a non-zero exit code (8) when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated that as a pass.

Let me look at their project files to understand the setup.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>

    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <NoDefaultTestingPlatformOptions>false</NoDefaultTestingPlatformOptions>
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>

    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <NoDefaultTestingPlatformOptions>false</NoDefaultTestingPlatformOptions>
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>"}

data.reasoningText: The `dotnet test` command is still failing. Let me check the log file to understand what's happening.

data.content: The `--minimum-expected-tests` doesn't accept `0`. But the log reveals a better option: `--ignore-exit-code 8`. Let me fix that.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <NoDefaultTestingPlatformOptions>false</NoDefaultTestingPlatformOptions>
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests discovered"; treat as success for conditionally-compiled assemblies -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText: The `--minimum-expected-tests` option doesn't accept 0 — it requires a "non-zero positive integer". But the log also shows the `--ignore-exit-code` option which can ignore specific exit codes. Exit code 8 is the "no tests found" exit code. Let me use `--ignore-exit-code 8` instead.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->

    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests discovered"; treat as success for conditionally-compiled assemblies -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content:
**The fix:** Add this MSBuild property to your `.csproj`:

```xml
<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
```

**What happened:** Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns **exit code 8** when zero tests are discovered (a safety feature to catch misconfigured projects). VSTest returned 0 in that case. The `--ignore-exit-code 8` flag tells the runner to map exit code 8 back to 0, restoring the VSTest behavior for your conditionally-compiled assemblies.

If you have multiple test projects affected, you can put this in a `Directory.Build.props` so it applies to all of them.
